name: NavCardPattern
description: Facade component that exposes both GNAV Nav Card patterns — promotional content and menu column — behind a single API. Uses NavCardShell for the shared frame and swaps in the promo/text or nav menu composition based on the pattern prop. Figma references the `Navigation / Nav Card Patterns` section, which now contains the standalone `Nav Card Promo` and `Nav Card Menu` component sets.

composedOf.config: Passes through all promo props (eyebrow, title, body, link CTA, CTA button).
Uses title + menuItems + CTA button props to render the nav list container pattern.

props.name: pattern
props.description: Selects which GNAV pattern to render from the combined component set.

props.name: title
props.description: Shared Title 4 heading. In promo mode it’s the hero title; in menu mode it labels the nav column.

props.name: eyebrow
props.description: Promo-only eyebrow text displayed above the title. Ignored for the menu pattern.

props.name: ctaLinkLabel
props.description: Promo-only text link label inside the card body.

props.name: ctaLinkHref
props.description: Promo-only link destination for the inline CTA.

props.name: ctaButtonLabel
props.description: Label for the pill CTA in both patterns.

props.name: ctaButtonHref
props.description: Destination for the pill CTA in both patterns.

props.name: menuItems
props.type: Array<{ label: string; href: string; state?: "default" | "hover" | "active"; showIconEnd?: boolean }>
props.description: Menu-only data for the stacked Link/Text atoms. Each entry maps to the nav list item pattern (default hover underline).

tokenFlags.suggestedToken: Primitive clamp until semantic nav column min exists
tokenFlags.note: Nav column minimum is dictated by GNAV layout. Documented with Primitive comment in NavCardShell.

layerNames..promo: NavCard promo composition (eyebrow, body, link, CTA)

dimensions.height: Auto — promo text or menu list drives height
dimensions.note: Both patterns share the same padding, CTA region, and CTA tokens so they present consistently in the GNAV flyout.

notes: Promo pattern follows the NavCard a11y contract (article region with link + button).
Menu pattern exposes stacked nav links inside a navigation region; ensure list order matches keyboard order.
